_lesson: Lesson 210: Finished Development Setup with webpack-dev-server.
_notes: The 'start' script was switched from 'lite-server' to 'webpack-dev-server'.
webpack-dev-server spins up a local server on port 8080 AND runs the
webpack build pipeline in watch mode. When source files change, the
bundle is automatically rebuilt and the browser refreshes.
IMPORTANT: webpack-dev-server keeps the bundle IN MEMORY — no file
is actually written to the dist folder while the dev server is running.
This is faster than disk writes but means the dist folder appears empty
during development. The webpack.config.js 'publicPath' setting tells
the dev server where to serve this in-memory bundle from.
The 'build' script (plain 'webpack') is unchanged — it still produces
real files in the dist folder for production deployment.
name: understanding-typescript
author: Maximilian Schwarzmüller
